claim_boundary: Tier 4.31a is local readiness/contract evidence only. A pass defines the smallest native fading-memory temporal-state subset, fixed-point equations, controls, readback schema, resource budget, command plan, and failure classes before any implementation or EBRAINS package. It does not prove C runtime implementation, SpiNNaker hardware transfer, speedup, multi-chip scaling, nonlinear recurrence, universal benchmark superiority, language, planning, AGI, or ASI.

command_plan.host_sdp_scope: setup, sham selection, compact readback only
command_plan.implementation_rule: do not add commands until 4.31b local fixed-point reference passes
command_plan.inter_core_scope: none in 4.31b; later MCPL only if temporal state must be shared across cores

controls.expected_effect: Lag-only should remain weaker on temporal-memory tasks or the temporal native migration should not be promoted.
controls.name: lag_only_online_lms_control
controls.purpose: Preserve the Tier 5.19c requirement that fading memory beat a same-causal-budget lag control on temporal-memory diagnostics.

controls.expected_effect: Zeroed traces should lose temporal-memory benefit.
controls.name: zero_temporal_state_ablation
controls.purpose: Proves task performance is not carried solely by current input/readout.

controls.expected_effect: Frozen state should not match the active temporal trace on held-out temporal tasks.
controls.name: frozen_temporal_state_ablation
controls.purpose: Matches the v2.2 sham where temporal state stops updating after the train/reference prefix.

controls.expected_effect: Shuffled state should lose temporal-memory benefit.
controls.name: shuffled_temporal_state_sham
controls.purpose: Destroys temporal ordering while preserving marginal state distribution.

controls.expected_effect: Short reset interval should reduce long-memory performance.
controls.name: state_reset_interval_control
controls.purpose: Checks whether the claimed memory horizon survives forced resets.

controls.name: shuffled_target_control
controls.purpose: Detects label/target leakage through the temporal path.

controls.expected_effect: No-plasticity path must not pass promoted learning claims.
controls.name: no_plasticity_ablation
controls.purpose: Separates temporal state availability from learning/readout adaptation.

controls.expected_effect: First native gate must not include hidden tanh recurrent units or recurrent weight matrices.
controls.name: hidden_recurrence_excluded_control
controls.purpose: Keeps Tier 4.31 aligned with the narrowed v2.2 claim.

criteria.name: nonlinear recurrence excluded
value: not language, planning, AGI, or ASI

criteria.name: smallest temporal subset declared
criteria.value: seven causal EMA traces over the current temporal input; deltas and novelty are derived, not stored

criteria.name: proposed command codes do not collide
no EBRAINS package prepared
criteria.note: This runner writes only controlled_test_output artifacts.

criteria.name: next step remains local
criteria.value: Tier 4.31b local fixed-point reference/parity; no EBRAINS package before that passes

equation_specs.clipping_rule: clip each trace to [-FP_ONE, FP_ONE] for first native gate
equation_specs.name: ema_trace_update
equation_specs.purpose: Own the v2.2 fading-memory state on chip using only bounded causal updates.

equation_specs.clipping_rule: clip only if emitted into a bounded summary/readout payload
equation_specs.name: trace_delta_feature
equation_specs.purpose: Preserve v2.2 multi-timescale contrast as a derived feature, not persistent state.

equation_specs.clipping_rule: clip emitted novelty to [-2 * FP_ONE, 2 * FP_ONE]
equation_specs.name: novelty_feature
equation_specs.purpose: Expose causal surprise/novelty without future target leakage.

equation_specs.clipping_rule: no hidden/tanh recurrent state in Tier 4.31b/4.31c
equation_specs.equation_fixed_point: readout consumes fixed-point current, trace, delta, and novelty fields only
equation_specs.name: temporal_readout_input
equation_specs.purpose: Keep the first native migration aligned with the narrowed v2.2 claim.

failure_classes.meaning: Host updates the temporal state outside the declared chip-owned path.
failure_classes.required_response: Fail the native claim and return to local source audit.

failure_classes.meaning: Temporal state depends on target/reward before it is causally available.
failure_classes.required_response: Fail and add shuffled-target/wrong-horizon diagnostics before retry.

failure_classes.meaning: Trace values clip or overflow under canonical inputs.
failure_classes.required_response: Fail 4.31b; reduce gain/range or widen state before hardware.

failure_classes.meaning: Same-budget lag control matches the temporal trace.
failure_classes.required_response: Do not promote native temporal migration; narrow claim.

failure_classes.meaning: Readback fields are missing, stale, or ambiguous.
failure_classes.required_response: Fail ingest; update schema version and parser before rerun.

failure_classes.meaning: Initial state/readback exceeds declared compact budget.
failure_classes.required_response: Fail readiness; redesign state subset before implementation.

failure_classes.meaning: Hidden recurrent units or recurrence-specific claims enter the first native gate.
failure_classes.required_response: Fail contract; separate into a later explicit recurrence tier.

failure_classes.meaning: Hardware upload is prepared before 4.31b local fixed-point parity passes.
failure_classes.required_response: Invalidate package and restore local-first sequence.

required_work: mirror the EMA trace update in local Python fixed-point
compare against the Tier 5.19c fading-memory reference on temporal-memory diagnostics
predeclare fixed-point tolerances and saturation limits
only then consider C source implementation and EBRAINS packaging
next_step.tier: Tier 4.31b - Native Temporal-Substrate Local Fixed-Point Reference

readback_schema.pass_rule: equals last scheduled input in local reference

state_specs.rationale: Version the readback parser and prevent stale report interpretation.
Smallest promoted v2.2 subset: bounded EMA traces only, no hidden recurrent state.
Make local 4.31b and C fixed-point equations auditable.
Avoid float-only decay behavior in the native claim path.
Proves the trace update used current/past input only.
state_specs.update_rule: last causal input used to update traces

state_specs.rationale: Carries the v2.2 novelty feature without storing an extra unbounded history.
Detects missed, duplicate, or host-side hidden updates.

state_specs.update_rule: increment on explicit reset/sham mode reset

state_specs.rationale: Prevents post-hoc relabeling of control runs.
state_specs.update_rule: selected by local reference or host command in future hardware probes

explicitly_excluded: Python-side temporal dictionaries in hardware claims
future target or reward in temporal state updates
subset_decision.next_gate: Tier 4.31b local fixed-point reference/parity; no EBRAINS package before that passes
subset_decision.state_subset: seven causal EMA traces over the current temporal input; deltas and novelty are derived, not stored
subset_decision.why: Tier 5.19c promoted fading_memory_only_ablation and explicitly did not promote bounded nonlinear recurrence. The smallest chip-owned subset is therefore the causal EMA trace bank plus compact counters/readback.
